summary: A solid soundboard capture of Jerry’s 40th‑birthday show; the hot‑stressed first set gives way to a standout Playin → Iko jam and strong second‑set flow.
review: The recording is a decent‑to‑excellent soundboard tape that preserves clear bass and crisp highs, though a few listeners note low‑level or occasional dropout issues.  Musically the band struggled through an overheated first set, but the second set shines with seamless segues—especially the celebrated Playin > Iko sequence, Lost Sailor > Saint of Circumstance, and tight drum work on Space/The Wheel.  Overall most fans remember this as a memorable birthday performance despite the heat‑induced fatigue early on.

ai_rating.rationale: The majority of reviews (≈70%) rate the show 4–5 stars and praise the performance, especially the second‑set jams; recording quality is consistently described as good to excellent, yielding an overall strong rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear mix with audible bass and crisp highs; a few comments about low‑level tape or minor drop‑outs but no major distortion. Overall considered listenable and better than audience recordings.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The scorching heat made the first set feel sluggish and strained, but once the band cooled down the second set launched into tight transitions—Playin → Iko, then Rider, Space/The Wheel, etc.—creating a cohesive, high‑energy flow.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Early heat left him visibly distressed and his solos sounded tentative; later he delivered brilliant, quirky licks on Playing/Iko and a celebrated Tennessee Jed solo.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass noted as clear and well‑defined in the soundboard tape; no specific performance critiques were mentioned.
band_member_comments.Bob: Made a humorous “lettin’ all the animals out” remark during Iko; vocal presence noted but not deeply evaluated.
band_member_comments.Drums: Drum work highlighted as a highlight of the second set, especially on Space and The Wheel.
